title: mark_note_spent silently succeeds for missing note_id
description:
db.rs:313-319 — UPDATE notes SET spent=1 WHERE note_id=? returns Ok(()) regardless of rows_affected. If the scanner calls this with a note_id that was already deleted, never inserted, or has an off-by-one, the call silently succeeds, the note is never marked spent, and unspent_notes() keeps returning it. The scanner will attempt to re-spend the same note on every scan cycle, producing incorrect balance calculations and potentially double-spend attempts.

**Why:** sqlx's execute() checks the DB operation succeeded, not that rows were affected. This is a common SQLite footgun.
**How to apply:** After execute, check rows_affected() > 0 and return an Err if zero.
close_reason: Fixed: mark_note_spent now checks rows_affected() and returns Err if zero. Added mark_note_spent_nonexistent_note_errors test.
